Corporation Counsel Judson has been
directed by Councilmen Doolttle and
Boyd of the Third ward to draft a
new ordinance extending the city fire
limits.


The proposed extension follows re-
quest from the lleiber Brewing COM
pany and other extensive property own
ers west of Monroe street, who feai
that n disastrous fire may result unless
the building of planning mills and wood
en- structures ends in the thickly built
up part of the city west of Madison
street and south of the river.


The present fire limits are Division
street on the east, the alley between
Second and Third avenues on the south
Madison street on. the west south Of
the river, Mallon avenue on the north
and Monroe street on the west north q
the .giver. The proposed extension will
be to Walnut street on the west soutl
of the river and south tc the North
crn Pacific railway tracks.


This would give an extension of five
blocks in the western part Of the city
Several valuable brick buildings hay,
been put up in this district within tn,
last two years and Others are to b,
built this year. IT Is the hope of tn
owners of these valuable Improvement
20 keep the fire risk down as low a
possible.
